response.justification: James Smith is a skilled polyglot developer with genuine strengths in high-throughput systems and event-driven architectures, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. C++ is essentially absent from his active skill set — his only documented C/C++ work was a six-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, and it does not even appear in his skills section. His career trajectory is firmly in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 cybersecurity practices, secure development lifecycle experience, MQTT or industrial protocol familiarity, or the multi-threaded systems-level C++ architecture this role demands.
strengths: Proven experience building high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby)
Consistent application of agile engineering practices — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development — across multiple senior roles
Event-driven architecture experience via GISCollective platform, demonstrating familiarity with the paradigm even if not in C++
concerns: C++ expertise is negligible: roughly six months of C/C++ work in 2014–2015 with no subsequent usage and absent from the skills section
No industrial automation, IoT, or relevant protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, OPC UA, DNP 3.0)
No demonstrated knowledge of secure development lifecycle, IEC 62443 standards, or any industrial cybersecurity practices
